narrative_thread: The wine glass is now on the nightstand, joining the other artifacts of your evening routine. The room feels slightly less chaotic, though Misos judgment remains unchanged.
blueprint: The immediate area consists of: 1) Your Bedroom (south from hallway), 2) The Hallway (current west exit), leading to 3) The Bathroom (current location, east from hallway), 4) The Kitchen (west from hallway), and 5) The Living Room (south from hallway, unexplored). The front door remains locked and alarmed in the living room.

player.description: You’re currently wearing a pair of faded blue flannel pajama pants with a small hole near the left knee and a stretched-out white tank top. The ensemble screams *‘I gave up’* more than it screams *‘professional.’* Miso’s judgmental gaze is almost palpable.

room_start.name: Your Bedroom
room_start.description:
...

You can see here: alarm clock, bed, nightstand, closet, dresser, Miso, pajamas.

items.name: wine glass
items.description: A stemless wine glass with a faint lipstick stain on the rim. It sits precariously on the nightstand, its sticky residue glistening in the morning light. The scent of last nights cabernet still lingers, a silent testament to your *relaxation* techniques. The glass looks out of place among the other clutter, like a relic of a less responsible era.

room_68f315ca.name: The Hallway
room_68f315ca.description:
The narrow hallway stretches before you, its walls a bland eggshell white that somehow manages to feel both sterile and lived-in. A thin strip of worn beige carpet muffles your footsteps, though it does little to absorb the faint scent of last night's takeout—something garlicky, now stale. To the east, a slightly ajar door reveals the tiled expanse of the bathroom, where the hum of an old exhaust fan lingers like a sleepy insect. To the west, the hallway opens into the kitchen, where the dim glow of the digital clock on the microwave casts a faint blue hue. A single framed photo of you and Miso, taken during a rare moment of feline cooperation, hangs crookedly on the wall, its glass smudged with what might be paw prints or your own fingerprints from a late-night snack run. The air here is cooler than in your bedroom, carrying the faintest whisper of central air and the lingering musk of Miso’s favorite napping spots.

You can see here: framed photo, beige carpet strip, hallway light, hoodie on hook.
room_68f315ca.base_description: The narrow hallway stretches before you, its walls a bland eggshell white that somehow manages to feel both sterile and lived-in. A thin strip of worn beige carpet muffles your footsteps, though it does little to absorb the faint scent of last night's takeout—something garlicky, now stale. To the east, a slightly ajar door reveals the tiled expanse of the bathroom, where the hum of an old exhaust fan lingers like a sleepy insect. To the west, the hallway opens into the kitchen, where the dim glow of the digital clock on the microwave casts a faint blue hue. A single framed photo of you and Miso, taken during a rare moment of feline cooperation, hangs crookedly on the wall, its glass smudged with what might be paw prints or your own fingerprints from a late-night snack run. The air here is cooler than in your bedroom, carrying the faintest whisper of central air and the lingering musk of Miso’s favorite napping spots.

room_7269d1b0.name: The Bathroom
room_7269d1b0.description:
The bathroom is a cramped but functional space, its once-white tiles now tinged with the faintest yellow from years of steam and hard water. The air is thick with the scent of damp towels and the sharp, antiseptic tang of mouthwash left uncapped on the sink. A flickering fluorescent light buzzes overhead, casting a harsh glow that does no favors to the dark circles under your eyes—visible in the mirror above the sink, which is streaked with toothpaste splatters and a single, deliberate smear of lipstick (a failed attempt at a night-out look). The exhaust fan hums weakly, struggling to dispel the humidity clinging to the shower curtain, which sways slightly as if someone—or something—just brushed past it. To the west, the hallway beckons, its cooler air a relief after the bathroom’s muggy embrace.

You can see here: mirror, damp towel, uncapped mouthwash, shower curtain.
room_7269d1b0.base_description: The bathroom is a cramped but functional space, its once-white tiles now tinged with the faintest yellow from years of steam and hard water. The air is thick with the scent of damp towels and the sharp, antiseptic tang of mouthwash left uncapped on the sink. A flickering fluorescent light buzzes overhead, casting a harsh glow that does no favors to the dark circles under your eyes—visible in the mirror above the sink, which is streaked with toothpaste splatters and a single, deliberate smear of lipstick (a failed attempt at a night-out look). The exhaust fan hums weakly, struggling to dispel the humidity clinging to the shower curtain, which sways slightly as if someone—or something—just brushed past it. To the west, the hallway beckons, its cooler air a relief after the bathroom’s muggy embrace.

alarm_clock.name: alarm clock
alarm_clock.description: A sleek, digital alarm clock with a cracked screen. It now displays 6:46 AM in red numbers, its beeping mercifully silenced. The snooze button is worn smooth from overuse, and the silence it’s granted you feels like a small victory.

bed.name: bed
bed.description: A queen-sized bed with a sagging mattress and a comforter that’s seen better days. The sheets are tangled, and the pillows are scattered. One corner of the bed is occupied by Miso, who regards you with the disdain of a monarch surveying a peasant.

nightstand.name: nightstand
nightstand.description: A small, wooden nightstand cluttered with the detritus of your life. There’s an empty water glass, a half-melted lavender-scented candle, and a well-worn copy of *The Duke’s Secret Bride* by your favorite romance author. A drawer is slightly open, revealing a tangle of charging cables and old receipts.

closet.name: closet
closet.description: A walk-in closet that’s more of a walk-*over* closet, given the state of its floor. Clothes hang haphazardly from hangers, some still in dry-cleaning bags, others discarded in piles. Shoeboxes teeter precariously on the top shelf, and a lone sock dangles from a doorknob. The air smells faintly of fabric softener and regret.

dresser.name: dresser
dresser.description: A sturdy oak dresser, its surface dusty and cluttered. A mirror hangs above it, reflecting your current state of disarray. The top drawer is slightly open, revealing a jumble of socks and underwear. The other drawers are closed, but you know from experience they’re no less chaotic inside.

miso.name: Miso
miso.description: Miso is a large, fluffy orange cat with a perpetually unimpressed expression. His green eyes regard you with a mix of disdain and mild curiosity, as if he’s already decided you’re not worth his time but hasn’t ruled out the possibility of food. His tail flicks lazily, and he’s currently sprawled across the foot of your bed like he owns the place.

pajamas.name: pajamas
pajamas.description: A faded set of blue flannel pants and a threadbare white tank top. The pants have a small hole near the left knee, and the tank top is stretched out from years of use. They’re comfortable, but they’re not exactly office-appropriate. The faint scent of fabric softener and last night’s wine clings to them.

phone.name: phone
phone.description: A slightly cracked smartphone with a battery life hovering at 12%. The lock screen displays a notification: *3 missed calls from Mom*. There’s also a text from your boss, sent at 6:30 AM: *‘Don’t forget the Thompson report is due today. No excuses.’* The phone feels heavier than usual in your hand.

wine_glass.name: wine glass
wine_glass.description: A stemless wine glass with a faint lipstick stain on the rim. The bottom is sticky with the remnants of last night’s cabernet, and there’s a smudge of your fingerprint on the side. It’s empty, but the scent of alcohol lingers, a reminder of your questionable life choices.

wine_glass_nightstand.name: wine glass
wine_glass_nightstand.description: A stemless wine glass with a faint lipstick stain on the rim. It sits precariously on the nightstand, its sticky residue glistening in the morning light. The scent of last nights cabernet still lingers, a silent testament to your *relaxation* techniques. The glass looks out of place among the other clutter, like a relic of a less responsible era.

item_hall_photo.name: framed photo
item_hall_photo.description: A 4x6 photo of you and Miso, taken during one of the few times he deigned to sit still for more than three seconds. You’re both squinting into the sunlight, Miso’s orange fur glowing like a small, judgmental ember. The frame is cheap plastic, the kind that comes with a pre-inserted stock photo you’re supposed to replace. You never did. The glass is smudged, and the back is slightly askew, as if Miso has taken it upon himself to investigate its contents at some point.

item_hall_rug.name: beige carpet strip
item_hall_rug.description: A thin, utilitarian strip of carpet that runs the length of the hallway. It’s seen better days—frayed at the edges, slightly discolored from years of foot traffic, and dotted with a few mysterious stains that you’ve long since given up trying to identify. It’s the kind of carpet that’s more functional than decorative, the kind that would be replaced if you ever got around to redecorating. Which you won’t.

item_hall_light.name: hallway light
item_hall_light.description: A single, unremarkable flush-mount ceiling light with a frosted glass cover. It’s the kind of light that’s bright enough to navigate the hallway but not so bright that it reveals every flaw in the paint job. The bulb flickers occasionally, a habit it’s had for years, though it’s never actually burned out. You’ve considered replacing it, but the thought of balancing on a chair to unscrew the cover while Miso watches with amused disdain has always deterred you.

item_hall_jacket.name: hoodie on hook
item_hall_jacket.description: A well-worn gray hoodie hangs from a hook on the wall, its sleeves slightly stretched from years of use. It smells faintly of fabric softener and the outdoors, though you can’t remember the last time you actually wore it outside. The pockets are empty, save for a single crumpled receipt from the corner store, dated three weeks ago. The hoodie is one of those comfort items you keep around for lazy weekends, though lately, it’s been serving more as a coat rack for Miso’s occasional attempts at climbing the walls.

item_mirror_001.name: mirror
item_mirror_001.description: The mirror is a standard-issue bathroom vanity model, its edges slightly fogged from years of use. Your reflection stares back at you—hair slightly disheveled, the remnants of last night’s mascara smudged under one eye. There’s a small chip in the lower left corner, a souvenir from the time Miso knocked your hair straightener into the sink. The glass is cool to the touch, and if you press your palm against it, you can feel the faint vibration of the exhaust fan behind the wall.

item_towel_001.name: damp towel
item_towel_001.description: A faded blue bath towel, still damp from your shower last night. It’s draped haphazardly over the edge of the tub, one corner trailing onto the floor where Miso has kneaded it into a lumpy mess. The fabric smells faintly of your shampoo—something floral and overpriced—and the underlying mustiness of a towel left too long in a humid room. There’s a small hole near the hem, likely from Miso’s claws.

item_mouthwash_001.name: uncapped mouthwash
item_mouthwash_001.description: A half-empty bottle of minty mouthwash, its cap nowhere to be seen. The liquid inside sloshes ominously as you pick it up, and the scent is overpowering—like a wintergreen bomb went off in your sinuses. The label is peeling at the edges, and someone (probably you) has scribbled "DO NOT DRINK" in permanent marker on the front, though the warning feels redundant given the alcohol content.

item_shower_curtain_001.name: shower curtain
item_shower_curtain_001.description: A cheap plastic shower curtain, its once-transparent surface now clouded with soap scum and hard water stains. It’s printed with a pattern of tiny ducks, a gift from your aunt who insisted it was "whimsical." The curtain sways slightly, as if disturbed by a draft, and the metal rings at the top creak when you tug on it. Behind it, the bathtub is visible—a porcelain relic with a chipped edge and a stubborn ring of grime around the drain.
